mechanism_narrative: Aggressive central bank tightening to combat high inflation causes a rapid increase in the federal funds rate. This shocks equity markets by increasing the discount rate for future earnings and raising fears of a policy-induced recession, leading to negative forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:As of May 2022, the FOMC has begun a rapid tightening cycle to address inflation running at multi-decade highs.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances following a monthly FEDFUNDS increase greater than 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: A persistently low unemployment rate below its natural rate indicates a tight labor market. This fuels wage-push inflation concerns, prompting expectations of future monetary tightening, which compresses equity multiples and leads to negative returns over a medium-term horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:The unemployment rate of 3.6% is near a 50-year low, indicating a very tight labor market.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the level of UNRATE and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where real GDP growth is negative while nominal retail sales remain positive indicates that inflation is eroding real activity and that consumer strength is illusory. This unsustainable dynamic foreshadows future earnings disappointments and negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth < 0% AND RSAFS 3-month growth > 0%
evidence_refs: fact:The advance estimate for Q1 2022 GDP showed a contraction, while consumer spending data has remained resilient.
failure_condition: In periods where quarterly real GDP contracts but 3-month retail sales grow, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in industrial production reflects a robust manufacturing and production sector. This is a direct indicator of strong corporate activity and output, which translates to better-than-expected earnings and drives positive short-term equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production has shown a consistent positive trend through early 2022.
failure_condition: The 3-month change in INDPRO has a negative rank information coefficient with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year inflation is already high, any further acceleration signals that price pressures are becoming entrenched. This forces the central bank into a more hawkish stance, increasing the probability of a hard landing and causing negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:The April 2022 CPI reading showed an 8.3% YoY increase, well above the trigger threshold.
failure_condition: When YoY CPI is above 5%, the S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a very low unemployment rate and continued strong payroll growth signals an overheating labor market. This condition virtually guarantees aggressive Fed tightening to combat wage inflation, leading to a risk-off sentiment in equity markets.
evidence_refs: fact:The labor market in early 2022 exhibits both sub-4% unemployment and strong job creation.
failure_condition: When the trigger conditions are met, the S&P 500 has positive 20-day forward returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts are a key leading indicator for the broader economy due to their sensitivity to interest rates and large multiplier effects. A sharp monthly decline signals a rapid cooling of economic activity, negatively impacting investor sentiment and near-term equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:The housing sector is widely expected to slow significantly as mortgage rates rise in 2022.
failure_condition: Instances of HOUST falling more than 5% in one month are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, positive labor market news (e.g., falling unemployment) is interpreted negatively by markets ('good news is bad news'). This is because a strong labor market gives the Fed more room to raise rates aggressively, increasing recession risk.
evidence_refs: fact:Market commentary in 2022 frequently cites strong jobs data as a reason for the Fed to tighten policy more aggressively.
regime_hint: monetary tightening with FEDFUNDS showing a positive 3-month trend
failure_condition: During periods of rising FEDFUNDS, a 3-month decrease in UNRATE is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of cases.
